factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran-Israel)
factors.summary: Iran and Israel agreed to halt missile strikes (BBC, Al Jazeera 23:17–23:25), reducing acute conflict premium. Oil steadied post-ceasefire; risk-off sentiment should ease. However, agreement is fragile with threat of retaliation if breached.

factors.name: Inflation Concerns & Fed Policy
factors.summary: US May CPI due Wednesday (tomorrow) is flagged as 'most consequential' ahead of June 17 FOMC; investinglive reports analyst consensus expects 'hot, hot, hot' print. ECB hiking to 2.25% Thursday signals global tightening bias. Market has priced near-certain 25bp hike by December.

factors.name: Oil Price & Energy Input Costs
factors.summary: API warns US Strategic Petroleum Reserve nearing critical low (~350M barrels), limiting government intervention capacity. Oil volatility elevated; higher fuel costs already downgrading JetBlue (S&P) and pressuring airlines. Input cost inflation risk rising.

factors.name: Dow Earnings & Forward Guidance
factors.summary: No major Dow component earnings catalysts in current news window. Multiple insider selling events (Texas Capital, Cathay General, Equinix officers) noted, but no forward guidance shifts from cyclical leaders (CAT, DE, HON, GE, BA) provided.

factors.name: Tech & AI Sector Dynamics
factors.summary: OpenAI confidentially files for IPO (expected Sept debut); SpaceX IPO imminent. Apple partners with Google/Nvidia on AI; Nvidia CEO endorses Qualcomm (+2%). AI capex cycle supports hardware and infrastructure names. However, BofA warns 70% of bear market signals triggered for S&P 500; tech valuation stretched.

factors.name: Market Breadth & Technical
factors.summary: Jim Cramer warns key pillars of bull market 'beginning to crumble.' BofA flags widest IT sector dispersion since Feb 2000 (pandemic-era volatility). Dow closed -0.16% (4h), S&P and NASDAQ closed slightly positive, indicating leadership breakdown and divergence.

factors.name: US Economic Data (Trailing)
factors.summary: UK retail sales surged in May (biggest annual rise since April 2025), but real purchasing power eroding below 3% inflation. US-specific data (durable goods, industrial production, ISM) not released in this window; Chinese trade data due Tuesday.

factors.name: USD Strength & Multinational Translation
factors.summary: GBP and JPY show mixed dynamics (Yen ignores strength drivers; Pound fragile). USD remains supported by Fed tightening narrative, but no fresh DXY catalyst in news. Dow has ~50% international revenue; stronger dollar headwind if sustained.

factors.name: 10Y Treasury Yield & Real Rates
factors.summary: Investinglive warns inflation-driven yield rise; rate-sensitive financials (JPM, GS, BAC) and dividend payers (JNJ, PG, KO) face headwind. No specific 10Y level provided, but 'yields rise' narrative dominates amid hot inflation expectations.

factors.name: Sector Rotation & Volatility
factors.summary: VIX backdrop unclear, but geopolitical flare-up + inflation fears + stretched tech valuations = choppy consolidation risk. Cramer's caution and BofA's bear signal tally suggest tactical pullback more likely than breakout.

screening.narrative: The Dow closed modestly lower (-0.16%) as mixed signals competed: Iran-Israel ceasefire reduced acute geopolitical premium, but looming hot US CPI (Wednesday) and ECB rate hike (Thursday) reignite inflation/tightening concerns. Tech leadership fractured (BofA flags 70% of bear signals triggered, widest sector dispersion since Feb 2000), and insider selling by corporate officers signals caution. Oil near critical SPR thresholds amplifies input-cost risk for cyclicals, while AI capex optimism (OpenAI/SpaceX IPOs, Apple-Google-Nvidia tie-ups) offers partial offset. The market is in PHASE 2—move down has begun, but hasn't extended sharply.
catalysts_ahead: US May CPI (Wednesday 2026-06-10, ~13:30 UTC) — consensus 'hot' print expected; hot surprise = bearish equities, bullish dollar
ECB Rate Decision & Statement (Thursday 2026-06-12) — 25bp hike fully priced, tone/forward guidance critical
FOMC Decision (June 17–18) — 25bp hike priced in; hawks vs. doves on terminal rate path
China May Trade Data (Tuesday 2026-06-09) — export/import growth; impacts Dow cyclicals (CAT, BA exposure to China demand)
US Durable Goods & Factory Orders (mid-week) — industrial production read, key for Dow composition
screening.risk_warning: CPI surprise to the cool side (deflationary shock) would invert bias to BULLISH and invalidate inflation concerns; geopolitical re-escalation (Iran-Israel) would spike VIX and oil, pressuring equities into CPI. Strong earnings surprise from a Dow heavyweight (JPM, UNH, MSFT) could reignite risk-on. Conversely, a dovish ECB/Fed pivot would support equities despite hot inflation (liquidity trumps rates in near term).
screening.entry_guidance: Move is underway (down -0.45% in 24h); await Wednesday CPI print before committing fresh shorts. On bounce to 50,900–51,000, consider tactical short with tight stop above 51,200. Longs risky into CPI; wait for clear break above 51,500 post-CPI to confirm reversal.
